assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 5-12: 'Epic Grouping Strategy: by-repository' with Epic 1 (TC-9006: trustify-backend) and Epic 2 (TC-9006: trustify-ui). Two Epics created, one per repository.

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 11-12: Epic 1 summary is 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', Epic 2 summary is 'TC-9006: trustify-ui'. Both follow the exact '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' format.

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Task Summary table (lines 100-110): Tasks 1,2,3,9 have Parent Epic 'TC-9006: trustify-backend' and Repository 'trustify-backend'; Tasks 4,5,6,7,8 have Parent Epic 'TC-9006: trustify-ui' and Repository 'trustify-ui'. All tasks specify their parent Epic and are correctly assigned by repository.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Incorporates links from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks) — evidenced by link decisions in the impact map or task output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 27-30: 'TC-9006 **Incorporates** Epic 1 (TC-9006: trustify-backend)' and 'TC-9006 **Incorporates** Epic 2 (TC-9006: trustify-ui)'. Line 30 explicitly states: 'Incorporates links go from the Feature to Epics, not from the Feature to individual Tasks.'

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are 1, 2, 3 (implementation) and 9 (documentation). Frontend tasks are 4, 5, 6, 7, 8. Backend implementation tasks (1-3) have lower numbers than all frontend tasks (4-8). Frontend tasks depend on backend tasks: Task 4 depends on Tasks 1,2; Task 5 depends on Task 4; Tasks 6,7,8 depend on Tasks 5-6. Task 9 (documentation) depends on all tasks 1-8. Dependency ordering is preserved.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates doc-only scope) and testing tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates cross-cutting testing scope) are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Non-doc/non-test tasks (1,2,4,5,6,7): All contain Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create and/or Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Testing tasks (3: integration-tests, 8: frontend-tests): Both contain Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements (also include Files to Create/Modify and Implementation Notes but that is not required). Documentation task (9: documentation): Contains Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements; exempt from Files to Modify/Create and Implementation Notes, which are correctly absent.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Epic creation with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key — evidenced by Epic creation parameters in the impact map or output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 9: table shows Issue Type 'Epic (level 1)' and Parent 'TC-9006'. Line 14: 'Epic creation uses the level-1 issue type named "Epic" with `parent` set to the feature issue key `TC-9006`.' Lines 18-20: 'issueTypeName: Epic, parent: TC-9006 (feature issue key)'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 task files contain a '## Repository' section with a single value: Tasks 1,2,3,9 have 'trustify-backend'; Tasks 4,5,6,7,8 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 task files contain: '## Target Branch' (all set to 'main'), '## Description' (substantive descriptions), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1 through task-9 output files.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks have separate digest files (task-1-digest.md through task-9-digest.md), each containing '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex-chars>'. Verified hashes are 64 lowercase hex characters: task-1: 545f4b218fc290c8118fe480c81e34aa7aabb774b177c422534a129971565f1c, task-2: 9cdb42dbc5d17a399a912c63d1ccc48b5cab5af30392906187e12445f39d3ac3, task-3: 9caa28549c412127b8de855cf663b8a528ab4f77c386897b2182c19c49599e19, task-4: 78bf1c72dad2adce38a721483d5eae15114a10b9a45f6434bce7e50bb37710f6, task-5: 88948f5983ae7db9f3772f315568a9779b5a8d44dcfd09a8d1c35e94d0cbbb97, task-6: 5af1d6bedd7a32e498baa8dcd10a4d083addddac65e7ec3d139058ff3c6cdd6c, task-7: 35e5626541be6b6616ea227725f2ec5d45fdcb43d801473f643858130f323b7a, task-8: 203fc47179d7a1d05ae3837be56b5fe9e5055a8cb8c6a938945097f85ac0e4a0, task-9: 749920dde507a6ab68ea3a31629d3eab683a6bbd2772fffe70e76a65cfac6f25. Also listed in summary-comment.md lines 87-96.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention annotations across tasks 1-8 use the prescribed format. Examples: Task 1 'Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/remediation/model/summary.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` module file scope.'; Task 4 'Applies: task modifies `src/api/rest.ts` matching the convention's `.ts` API file scope.'; Task 7 'Applies: task modifies `src/routes.tsx` matching the convention's `.tsx` routing file scope.' No 'Not applicable' annotations found. No free-form prose rationales. Each rationale names a specific file from Files to Modify or Files to Create. Task 9 (documentation, no code files listed) has no convention annotations, correctly excluding code-scoped conventions.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 93: 'Priority: Major (inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to all Epics and Tasks)'. Lines 22-23: Epic creation includes 'priority: {"name": "Major"} (inherited from Feature TC-9006)'. summary-comment.md line 62: priority | Major | All Epics and Tasks | 'Inherited from Feature TC-9006. Priority is "Major" (not "Undefined"), so it is propagated via additional_fields.' Feature priority is Major (not Undefined), and inheritance is documented.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 94: 'fixVersions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to all Epics and Tasks; fixVersion scope defaults to "both" as no Jira Field Defaults section exists in CLAUDE.md)'. Lines 22-23: Epic creation includes 'fixVersions: [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}] (inherited from Feature TC-9006)'. summary-comment.md line 63: 'No `fixVersion scope` setting exists in CLAUDE.md Jira Field Defaults, so the default scope "both" applies — fixVersions are propagated to both Epics and Tasks.' Feature has non-empty fixVersions, scope is absent (defaults to 'both'), and inheritance is documented.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists with 'Inherited Field Propagation' table (lines 59-64) documenting: priority=Major propagated to all Epics and Tasks, fixVersions=RHTPA 1.5.0 propagated to all Epics and Tasks, labels=ai-generated-jira applied to all issues. Also impact-map.md 'Field Inheritance' section (lines 92-94) with same information. Both include the specific values propagated.
